title: Clippy: 4 assigning_clones — use Clone::clone_from for re-use
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Performance & Complexity
**Lint:** clippy::assigning_clones

## Violation
`x = y.clone()` when `x` already exists allocates a new value and drops the old one. `x.clone_from(&y)` reuses `x`'s allocated buffer (for `String`, `Vec`, etc.), avoiding allocator churn.

## Locations
- src/tui/app.rs: 707, 708, 709, 710 (cluster — likely a state-copy block)

## Mandated Refactor
```rust
// Before
self.status = other.status.clone();
// After
self.status.clone_from(&other.status);
```
Four consecutive lines strongly suggest a state-update block — consider extracting a `clone_from_other(&mut self, other: &Self)` helper method to encapsulate the field-by-field copy.
